pi_items.source: Downloaded imagej.net/images/mitosis.tif and verified it is a 5D hyperstack (51 frames, 5 slices, 2 channels) of Drosophila S2 cell mitosis.
Python image processing of C1 max projection at Frame 30 reveals ~5.85 um between spindle poles (based on XResolution 11.3 px/um). 4.0 um is a valid lower bound.
Python image processing of C1 max projection at Frame 30 reveals ~5.85 um between spindle poles. 9.0 um is a valid upper bound.
Python image processing of C1 max projection at Frame 45 reveals ~8.68 um between spindle poles. 8.0 um is a valid lower bound.
Python image processing of C1 max projection at Frame 45 reveals ~8.68 um between spindle poles. 14.0 um is a valid upper bound.
With finterval 0.142857 (~8.5s) per frame, 15 frames is 2.14 min. Elongation of ~2.83 um / 2.14 min = 1.32 um/min. 0.3 is a valid minimum.
Calculated elongation velocity of ~1.32 um/min properly falls below the 2.5 maximum limit.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real, built-in Fiji 'Mitosis' 5D hyperstack (Drosophila S2 cell). The requested output paths for the spindle projection image and the text report exactly match the bash export scripts. Quantitative analysis using Python confirms that at Frame 30 the pole-to-pole distance in the Green (C1) channel is approximately 5.85 um, and at Frame 45 it elongates to roughly 8.68 um. Over the 15-frame interval (which corresponds to 2.14 minutes based on the embedded 0.142857 min/frame metadata), the resulting velocity is roughly 1.32 um/min. All values correctly fall within the expected min/max metadata ranges and verifier logic constraints.
